label: Friendly SaaS
description: Approachable SaaS marketing variant with softer product framing and growth clarity.
signatureMotif: friendly product framing, rounded proof blocks, and accessible SaaS clarity
promptHints: Balance product proof with approachable copy and visual warmth.
Pricing and product trust should be clear without becoming enterprise-stiff.

layouts: Use a two-column hero with warm headline copy on the left and a rounded product screenshot card with floating stat chips on the right.
Build feature sections as alternating 55/45 splits, pairing short benefit copy with soft-framed UI panels and small proof callouts.
Compose social proof as a row of rounded testimonial or logo cards directly beneath the hero, keeping trust visible before deep product detail.
Use a three-tier pricing grid with the middle plan gently raised, stronger primary action, and concise comparison bullets beneath each tier.
Frame the final CTA as a centered rounded panel with short reassuring copy, a single primary button, and lightweight trust notes underneath.
motifs: Cards use 1rem corner radius, pale borders, and soft shadows so every product or proof block feels approachable rather than sharp-edged.
Primary actions appear as solid violet buttons against bright light surfaces, with mint or cool-tint accents reserved for supportive highlights and badges.
Product visuals sit inside white device-like frames with subtle layered depth, often accompanied by floating metrics, avatars, or success toasts.
Sections favor airy spacing, short copy blocks, and rounded proof elements that keep clarity high without drifting into enterprise dashboard density.
antiPatterns: Avoid full-bleed dark hero sections with neon gradients; they make the product feel too aggressive for this warm, clear SaaS variant.
Do not use dense comparison tables or procurement-style pricing language that pushes the page into enterprise-stiff territory.
Avoid razor-sharp card corners, heavy strokes, or brutalist layouts; this variant depends on softness and approachable product framing.
Never overwhelm the hero with multiple dashboard screenshots or complex collage stacks that compete with the main conversion message.
